title: asperula glomerata subsp. hirsutiuscula
intro: Asperula glomerata subsp. hirsutiuscula, also known as Woodruff, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can be attributed to the presence of iridoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Woodruff include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Woodruff is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome.
